gloss.en: public attention given by media

words.example: The film got a lot of publicity.

gloss.en: to make something widely known

words.example: They publicize the event on social media.

gloss.en: business of preparing and selling books

words.example: She has a job in publishing.

gloss.en: a sweet dish eaten after a meal

words.example: We had chocolate pudding for dessert.

gloss.en: a large round orange vegetable

words.example: They carved a face into the pumpkin.

gloss.en: arriving or doing things on time

words.example: She is always punctual for meetings.

gloss.en: the quality of being on time

words.example: Punctuality is important at work.

gloss.en: loud, aggressive rock music from the 1970s

words.example: He plays in a punk band.

gloss.en: a child being taught at school

words.example: Each pupil has their own desk.

gloss.en: the act of buying something

words.example: She made a large purchase online.

gloss.en: not mixed with anything else

words.example: This ring is made of pure gold.

gloss.en: only; completely

words.example: I saw the letter purely by chance.

gloss.en: having a clear aim; determined

words.example: She walked in with a purposeful look.

gloss.en: to try to achieve something over time

words.example: He wants to pursue a career in music.

gloss.en: the act of trying to get something

words.example: They share the pursuit of happiness.

gloss.en: a game you solve by thinking

words.example: He solved the crossword puzzle quickly.

gloss.en: confused because you cannot understand

words.example: She gave me a puzzled look.

gloss.en: loose clothes worn for sleeping

words.example: He put on his pyjamas before bed.

gloss.en: an exam passed or course completed

words.example: She has a teaching qualification.

gloss.en: to measure or express as a number

words.example: It is hard to quantify the damage.

gloss.en: separation to stop disease spreading

words.example: The dog was kept in quarantine.

gloss.en: an angry argument or disagreement

words.example: They had a quarrel over money.

gloss.en: tending to argue with people

words.example: He becomes quarrelsome when tired.

gloss.en: strange or unusual

words.example: There was a queer smell in the room.

gloss.en: a list of questions to collect information

words.example: Please fill in this questionnaire.

gloss.en: to leave your job or stop doing something

words.example: If I don't get more money I'll quit.

gloss.en: worth quoting; memorable

words.example: The speech was full of quotable lines.

gloss.en: words taken from a book or speech

words.example: He began with a quotation from Shakespeare.

gloss.en: a short piece taken from a text

words.example: The essay was full of quotes.

gloss.en: relating to different races of people

words.example: The law fights racial discrimination.

gloss.en: unfair treatment based on race

words.example: She was a victim of racism.

gloss.en: someone who treats races unfairly

words.example: He made racist remarks online.

gloss.en: a frame for holding things

words.example: She hung her coat on the rack.

gloss.en: to send out heat, light or energy

words.example: The fire began to radiate warmth.

gloss.en: dangerous rays from radioactive material

words.example: High levels of radiation can harm cells.

gloss.en: in a complete or extreme way

words.example: The plan is radically different now.

gloss.en: a radioactive metal element

words.example: Marie Curie discovered radium.

gloss.en: a sloping beam supporting a roof

words.example: Birds nested in the rafter above.

gloss.en: a piece of old, torn cloth

words.example: He wiped his hands on a rag.

gloss.en: a bar used as a barrier or support

words.example: She leaned on the ship's rail.

gloss.en: a curved band of colours in the sky

words.example: A rainbow appeared after the rain.

gloss.en: a storm with heavy rain

words.example: A sudden rainstorm flooded the street.

gloss.en: a male sheep

words.example: The ram has large curved horns.

gloss.en: a slope joining two different levels

words.example: He pushed the cart up the ramp.

gloss.en: chosen without any regular pattern

words.example: We picked a random number.

gloss.en: a variety of things of one type

words.example: The hotel offers a wide range of facilities.

gloss.en: a person who looks after a park or forest

words.example: A park ranger guided the tour.

gloss.en: a position in an organization or society

words.example: He holds a high rank in the army.

gloss.en: happening quickly

words.example: The city saw rapid growth.

gloss.en: very quickly

words.example: The disease spread rapidly.

gloss.en: acting too quickly without thinking

words.example: Don't make a rash decision.

gloss.en: a small animal like a large mouse

words.example: A rat ran across the floor.

gloss.en: the speed at which something happens

words.example: The rate of inflation fell again.

gloss.en: a measure of how good or popular something is

words.example: The show has a high rating.

gloss.en: a baby's toy that makes a noise

words.example: The baby shook the rattle.

gloss.en: to badly damage or destroy something

words.example: The fire ravaged the whole forest.

gloss.en: not cooked

words.example: He doesn't like raw meat.

gloss.en: the distance you can stretch to touch

words.example: The shot was beyond the reach of the goalkeeper.

gloss.en: what you do as a result of something

words.example: His reaction surprised everyone.

gloss.en: the moment you understand something

words.example: The realisation hit her suddenly.

gloss.en: sensible about what is actually possible

words.example: We need a realistic plan.

gloss.en: fair, sensible and practical

words.example: That sounds like a reasonable price.

gloss.en: to a fairly good degree

words.example: The instructions are reasonably clear.

gloss.en: refusing to obey rules or authority

words.example: He was a rebellious teenager.

gloss.en: to build something again

words.example: They began to rebuild their homes.

gloss.en: to remember something

words.example: She could not recall his name.

gloss.en: the part of a phone you hold

words.example: He picked up the receiver to answer.

gloss.en: a period of weak economic activity

words.example: Many firms closed during the recession.

gloss.en: instructions for cooking a dish

words.example: This is a recipe for chicken soup.

gloss.en: to think or have an opinion

words.example: I reckon I'm going to get that job.

gloss.en: the act of knowing who someone is

words.example: There was no sign of recognition in his eyes.

gloss.en: to remember something

words.example: I can't recollect what she said.

gloss.en: a memory of something

words.example: I have no recollection of that day.

gloss.en: good enough to be recommended

words.example: This restaurant is highly recommendable.

gloss.en: an official suggestion about what to do

words.example: We accepted the committee's recommendation.

gloss.en: to think again about a decision

words.example: Please reconsider your decision.

gloss.en: to get well again after illness

gloss.en: the process of getting well again

gloss.en: activities done for enjoyment and rest

words.example: The park is used for recreation.

gloss.en: to find new people to join a group

words.example: The company wants to recruit more staff.

gloss.en: the process of finding new members

words.example: Recruitment of new staff has begun.

gloss.en: shaped like a rectangle

words.example: The table is rectangular.

gloss.en: to do something again

words.example: I had to redo the whole report.

gloss.en: the act of making something smaller

words.example: There was a reduction in prices.

gloss.en: no longer needed or useful

words.example: Hundreds of workers were made redundant.

gloss.en: a tall plant growing near water

words.example: Ducks hid among the tall reed.

gloss.en: the official who controls a sports game

words.example: He argued with the referee.

gloss.en: a mention of someone or something

words.example: She made no reference to her illness.

gloss.en: the thing a word stands for

words.example: A pronoun needs a clear referent.

gloss.en: a factory that purifies oil or sugar

words.example: They work at an oil refinery.

gloss.en: thinking deeply; or sending back light

words.example: She was in a quiet, reflective mood.

gloss.en: to stop yourself from doing something

words.example: Please refrain from smoking here.

gloss.en: to make someone feel less tired

words.example: A cold drink will refresh you.

gloss.en: a person forced to leave their country

words.example: Many a refugee fled the war zone.

gloss.en: to prove that something is wrong

words.example: She tried to refute the claim.

gloss.en: care or attention for something

words.example: He showed little regard for safety.

gloss.en: a type of music from Jamaica

words.example: They danced to reggae all night.

gloss.en: relating to a particular region

words.example: The service is available at a regional level.

gloss.en: an official list or record of names

words.example: The bride and groom signed the register.

gloss.en: the act of recording something officially

words.example: Registration for the course is now open.

gloss.en: a sad feeling about something done

words.example: She felt a pang of regret.

gloss.en: unfortunately; in a way to be sorry about

words.example: Regrettably, the trip was cancelled.

gloss.en: to control something with rules

words.example: Laws regulate the insurance industry.

gloss.en: an official rule made by an authority

words.example: There are too many rules and regulations.

gloss.en: practice of a play before a performance

words.example: The band had a final rehearsal.

gloss.en: to make something stronger

words.example: The experience reinforced my sense of loss.

gloss.en: the act of making something stronger

words.example: The wall needs reinforcement.

gloss.en: to join something again

words.example: She decided to rejoin the team.

gloss.en: connected with something in some way

words.example: The two events are closely related.

gloss.en: to a fairly large degree by comparison

words.example: The test was relatively easy.

gloss.en: the physics theory about space and time

words.example: Einstein developed the theory of relativity.

gloss.en: a way of resting and enjoying yourself

words.example: Reading is my favourite relaxation.

gloss.en: a race between teams of runners

words.example: Our team won the relay.

gloss.en: closely connected with the subject

words.example: Please give only relevant information.

gloss.en: an old object surviving from the past

words.example: The museum holds an ancient relic.

gloss.en: happy feeling when something bad stops

words.example: I felt a huge sense of relief.

gloss.en: to reduce pain or an unpleasant feeling

words.example: This medicine will relieve the pain.

gloss.en: happy because something bad has stopped

words.example: She sounded relieved.

gloss.en: to depend on or trust someone

words.example: You can rely on me for help.

gloss.en: still left after others are gone
